Issues tagged with Sandbox-Issues label

<https://linear.app/testbox/issue/PLA-3366/brevo-deal-custom-field-attribute-keys-dont-match-brevos-internal-keys|PLA-3366 [Brevo] Deal custom field attribute keys don't match Brevo's internal keys for FR/DE accounts>

When creating deal custom fields on French and German accounts, the labels contain accented characters (e.g., `"Budget confirmé"`, `"Budget bestätigt"`). We send the label to Brevo via POST, and ​*Brevo generates the*​ `internalName` on their side — we don't control it. Our code then needs this `internalName` to activate the field in attribute groups and to PATCH deal attributes.

Currently, we predict the key locally using `_normalize_attribute_key` instead of reading it from Brevo's API. This breaks on ​*old accounts*​ because Brevo changed their internal normalization logic:

•  ​*Old Brevo normalization*​: stripped accents entirely → `"Budget confirmé"` → `budget_confirm`
•  ​*New Brevo normalization*​: transliterates accents → `"Budget confirmé"` → `budget_confirme`
Our current `_normalize_attribute_key` produces `budget_confirme`, which matches new accounts but fails on old accounts that have `budget_confirm` stored.
This causes two failures on old FR/DE accounts:

1.  ​*Activation fails*​ — `ingest_deals_custom_fields` creates the fields successfully but fails when trying to activate them in the attribute group (`"validate attributes: invalid attribute"`)
2.  ​*Deal update fails*​ — `ingest_new_deal` tries to PATCH deal attributes with the wrong key (`"Invalid attribute: budget_confirme passed"`)

•  ​*Root cause*​: We guess the attribute key with `_normalize_attribute_key` instead of reading the actual `internalName` from Brevo's API response or attributes endpoint.
•  ​*Old accounts*​: Brevo generated `budget_confirm` (old normalization). Our code tries `budget_confirme`. Mismatch.
•  ​*New accounts*​: Brevo generates `budget_confirme` (current normalization). Our code tries `budget_confirme`. Works fine.
•  ​*English accounts*​: Unaffected. `"Budget Confirmed"` → `budget_confirmed` — no accented characters, same key regardless.
`competitor` and `urgency` don't have accents so Brevo's normalization produces the same key regardless of era. Only `budget_confirmed` is affected in FR and DE.

Read the actual `internalName` from Brevo's deal attributes API (`GET /v3/crm/attributes/deals`) instead of computing it with `_normalize_attribute_key`. This follows the same pattern already used for company attributes (line ~2573 in `brevo_service.py`). This handles both old and new accounts automatically since we use whatever key Brevo actually has stored.

text.text: <https://linear.app/testbox/issue/PLA-3266/brevo-deals-close-date-needs-to-be-updated|PLA-3266 [Brevo] Deals close date needs to be updated>

Currently, we create deals in Brevo with a close date set at deal creation. However, when we later move these deals to a "Closed Won" or "Closed Lost" stage, the close date still reflects the original date, which may be earlier or later than the actual closing date. This creates data inconsistencies, as it appears deals were closed on dates that don't align with reality.
To resolve this, we need to update the close date to today ​*before*​ moving any deal to a closed stage. This ensures the close date always accurately reflects the day the deal was actually closed.
​*Rule*​: Always update the deal's close date to the current date before closing the deal.

•  What this change is about: Updating the close date for all deals to match the actual closing date before transitioning them to a closed stage.
•  Context: Deals currently show inaccurate close dates because the date is set at creation and never updated when the deal is closed.
•  Why: Accurate close dates are essential for reporting, pipeline analytics, and ensuring data integrity in the Brevo demo environment.

1.  Before moving any deal to a closed stage (Closed Won / Closed Lost), the deal's close date must be updated to the current date (today).
2.  This applies to all deals in the pipeline, regardless of when they were originally created.
3.  The close date update must occur before the stage change, not after.

•  When a deal moves to the closed stage, its close date reflects the exact date it was closed (today's date).
•  No deal exists in a closed stage if the close date differs from the day it was actually closed.
•  The close date update occurs before the stage transition.

text.text: <https://linear.app/testbox/issue/PLA-3309/tropic-fix-departments-purchase-category-and-savings-amount-on-tropic|PLA-3309 [Tropic] Fix Departments, Purchase Category and Savings Amount on Tropic Dataset>

The ​*Savings Amount*​ and ​*Savings Type*​ fields are not being correctly set on active software contracts in the Tropic dataset. This issue was first identified in December 2025 and is linked to incorrect vendor classification (e.g., Adobe tagged as "Events" instead of "Software"), which prevents the savings opportunity from being calculated.
Jordana (Tropic) confirmed the business rules in December 2025:

•  ​*All contracts must have a Savings Type*​ — blank/empty is not acceptable.
•  If ​*Savings Type = "None"*​, then ​*Savings Amount = "$0"*​ (not "not set", not blank).

As of February 2026, newly ingested trial instances (53/54) still show savings opportunity as "not set" for all contracts. Diego confirmed this is an ingestion issue — the correct data exists in the source files but is not being properly reflected after ingestion.

Previous fixes were applied via `suppliers_fixed.xlsx` and `tropic_new_dept_savings.xlsx`, but the corrections are not fully reflected. This ticket covers ​*fixing the source dataset only*​. Follow-up tickets will be created to propagate the fix to demo and sandbox environments.
:pushpin: ​*Feature Page:*​ N/A — This is a data fix, not a feature implementation.

•  Fix the Savings Type and Savings Amount values in the Tropic source dataset.
•  Ensure all contracts have a valid, non-blank Savings Type assigned.
•  Ensure contracts with Savings Type = "None" have Savings Amount = "$0".
•  Correct any remaining vendor/supplier classification issues that affect savings calculations (e.g., Adobe must be "Software", not "Events").
•  Document all changes made (list of records modified and what was changed).

•  Every contract in the dataset has a non-blank Savings Type.
•  Contracts with Savings Type = "None" have Savings Amount = "$0".
•  Vendor classifications are correct (no software vendors tagged as other categories).
•  Changes are documented with a changelog file (records modified and values changed).

•  Link related tickets that are related to this task in Linear.
•  Add ticket relationships — this ticket ​*blocks*​ the follow-up demo and sandbox fix tickets.
•  Add to a milestone.
•  Priority: ​*Medium*​.
•  ​*Follow-up tickets needed:*​ (1) Demo environment fix, (2) Sandbox environment fix.
